config_workspace_hint: These preferences are saved in the active workspace and applied immediately.
config_language_hint: Language affects menus, panels, and application dialogs.
config_appearance_system_note: Appearance (light or dark) follows your operating system settings automatically.
config_dev_mode_hint: Unlocks advanced database tools and other admin features. Requires a password.
config_dev_mode_active_hint: Development mode is active. Full access to settings, maintenance, table explorer, and SQL console.
config_dev_lock_closed_tip: Tools are locked. Click to enter the administrator password.
config_dev_lock_open_tip: Development mode is active. Click to lock again.
config_dev_password_intro: Enter the administrator password to enable advanced tools.
config_db_intro: Global SQLite settings. Stored in application configuration (not per workspace). Some changes require reconnecting.
config_db_user_intro: Summary of the local database. Show data lives in the project (.crf); SQLite keeps an indexed copy of the RF inventory.
config_db_dev_intro: Administrator tools: connection, maintenance, table explorer, and SQL console.
help_manual_intro: Operator guide: projects, inventory, Monitor, and everyday shortcuts.
help_supervision_tip: RF supervision guide, alarms, and Monitor F1–F10 shortcuts
app_update_headline: A new version is available ({latest}). You have {current}.
app_update_check_tip: Check GitHub for a newer release
app_update_none: You already have the latest version ({version}).
app_update_error: Could not check for updates. Verify your connection or GitHub settings.
cal_offline_unavailable_packaged: Offline matrix is not available in the installed build.
project_none_hint: Create a new project or open an existing .crf file.
project_file_unsaved_hint: This document has not been saved to disk yet.
inventario_rf_panel_lista_placeholder: RF equipment list — not implemented yet.
inventario_rf_panel_propiedades_placehol: Selected equipment properties — not implemented yet.
monitor_spectrum_waiting: Press Start in SOURCE to view the spectrum.
monitor_marker_config_intro: Choose what appears on the vertical marker line (click the graph label).
monitor_marker_auto_pan: Auto-pan: recenter window when F leaves the screen
monitor_cfg_fuente_play_hint: Use ▶ PLAY on the toolbar to start or stop capture.
monitor_cfg_debug_intro: Driver installation, USB checks and advanced diagnostics.
monitor_cfg_debug_cal_intro: Guided calibration wizard: applies each test, you validate visually and record PASS/FAIL. Report at logs/calibration/wizard_latest.json
cal_wizard_intro: Follow steps in order. «Apply configuration» sets the device; backend analyzes the full chain. If something fails, describe what you see before marking Fail.
cal_wizard_intro_short: Calibration checklist — select a step on the left.
cal_wizard_launcher_hint: The checklist opens in a large resizable window so you can write comments and view the spectrum at the same time.
cal_wizard_backend_placeholder: Press «Apply» or «Re-analyze» for the RF chain report…
cal_wizard_comment_placeholder: Describe what happens: scale, trace, RBW, SPAN, IQ/sweep mode, noise, flicker, errors…
cal_wizard_comment_required_hint: Backend FAIL — describe what you see before marking Fail
cal_wizard_comment_required: Enter what failed in the comment box before marking Fail.
cal_wizard_need_play: Press ▶ PLAY before applying this step.
cal_wizard_complete: Calibration done: {pass_n} OK, {fail_n} fails. See logs/calibration/wizard_latest.json
cal_step_prep_device_body: Verify HackRF appears under DEVICE and USB status is OK.
cal_step_prep_play_body: Press ▶ PLAY. Wait 2–3 s until the trace moves without status errors.
cal_step_prep_play_observe: Live trace, no capture_error or endless restarts.
cal_step_iq_span_10m_body: Analyzer set to 10 MHz with AUTO FFT/RBW. Must use continuous IQ (not sweep).
cal_step_iq_span_10m_observe: Status: IQ mode. SPAN ≈ 10 MHz. Derived RBW. SWT shows «—».
cal_step_iq_trace_body: With signal present, evaluate stability and noise floor.
cal_step_iq_trace_observe: Continuous trace; peaks at known frequencies; readable AUTO scale.
cal_step_iq_fft_body: FFT manual 4096. RBW should narrow (more bins → lower RBW).
cal_step_iq_fft_observe: FFT=4096 on toolbar. RBW ≈ SR/4096. Finer frequency detail.
cal_step_iq_rbw_body: Manual RBW 50 kHz recalculates FFT. IQ only.
cal_step_iq_rbw_observe: RBW ~50 kHz; FFT adjusted; coherent trace without stream reset.
cal_step_iq_20m_body: HackRF max IQ SPAN. Must stay IQ with sample_rate ≈ 20 MHz.
cal_step_iq_20m_observe: IQ mode, 20 MHz SPAN, trace fills width. SWT should read «N/A» — expected, not editable.
cal_step_trans_19_21_body: Critical crossing: above 20 MHz must switch to sweep.
cal_step_trans_19_21_observe: At 21 MHz: sweep mode, RBW ≥ 100 kHz, stable panoramic trace.
cal_step_sweep_50m_observe: Fluid sweep; RBW and SWT non-zero; FFT = display grid.
cal_step_sweep_rbw_body: Manual RBW 300 kHz freezes SWT (must not go AUTO or 0).
cal_step_sweep_rbw_observe: RBW=300 kHz, fixed SWT visible, slightly coarser trace.
cal_step_sweep_fft_body: Manual FFT 1024 — display grid only, not hardware RBW.
cal_step_sweep_fft_observe: FFT=1024; hardware RBW stable; no artifacts.
cal_step_hyst_18m_body: After wide sweep, 18 MHz should stay in sweep (FM stability).
cal_step_hyst_18m_observe: Still sweep at 18 MHz (not IQ yet).
cal_step_hyst_10m_body: At 10 MHz should restore fluid IQ.
cal_step_hyst_10m_observe: IQ mode, continuous trace, RBW from FFT.
cal_step_ref_auto_body: AUTO reference and range. Full trace visible with good contrast.
cal_step_ref_auto_observe: Ref/range adapt; peaks not clipped; floor visible.
cal_step_ref_manual_observe: Fixed scale; ref changes don't restart capture.
cal_step_gain_lna_body: Toggle LNA 16↔32 dB. Stream must not drop.
cal_step_gain_lna_observe: Trace level shifts ~6 dB; no involuntary STOP.
cal_step_gain_vga_body: Toggle VGA 16↔32 dB with same criteria.
cal_step_gain_vga_observe: Smooth level change; no long spectrum flicker.
monitor_cfg_panel_collapse_tip: Minimize side panel (more space for spectrum)
monitor_waterfall_panel_collapse_tip: Minimize waterfall panel (more space for spectrum)
monitor_cfg_markers_intro: Markers M1–M10. In F mode, drag the label box (⇔) to move. Double-click opens settings. On = enabled; Ln = vertical line.
monitor_markers_on_tip: On: enables the marker (ΔF, peak search, etc.). Off = hidden, no measurement.
monitor_markers_line_tip: Ln: show or hide the marker vertical line on spectrum and waterfall.
monitor_markers_freq_show_tip: Fr: show frequency in the on-screen marker label.
monitor_markers_level_show_tip: Lv: show level in the on-screen marker label.
monitor_markers_snr_show_tip: S/N: show signal/noise ratio in the on-screen label.
monitor_markers_delta_ref_missing: Enable at least one other marker (e.g. M1) before using Delta mode.
monitor_markers_delta_ref_tip: Enabled reference marker for ΔF and Δ level
monitor_cfg_markers_placeholder: Marker table and modes (Normal, Delta, Peak) — coming soon.
monitor_cfg_freq_manager_placeholder: Inventory markers and frequency manager — coming soon.
monitor_cfg_vfo_colors_placeholder: VFO and marker colors — coming soon.
monitor_cfg_display_placeholder: Colormap, grid and units — coming soon.
monitor_freq_mode_fc_tip: FC mode: center frequency fixed at spectrum center (blue)
monitor_freq_mode_f_tip: F mode: movable tune frequency on spectrum (orange)
monitor_cfg_alarmas_placeholder: Thresholds and alarm manager — coming soon.
monitor_freq_manager_intro: Inventory channels under supervision. Only the Supervised column is editable; the spectrum vertical band disappears when disabled. Resizable, sortable columns (saved in project).
monitor_freq_manager_summary_refs: {enabled} of {total} channels supervised · {refs} with fixed reference.
monitor_freq_manager_bulk_preset_tip: Threshold preset for quick assignment to all channels.
monitor_freq_manager_bulk_preset_apply: Apply selected preset to all channels
monitor_freq_manager_mode_tip: vs noise: SNR above local floor · vs reference: drop from on-air fixed level
monitor_freq_manager_empty: No channels in inventory. Import or create equipment in RF Inventory.
monitor_freq_manager_bw_tip: Measurement bandwidth. Default from device type; editable (manual override).
monitor_freq_manager_col_thresholds_tip: Alarm threshold preset for this channel (Normal, Strict, or custom).
monitor_freq_manager_snr_tip: SNR warning/critical thresholds (dB above noise). Format: 6.0 / 3.0
monitor_preset_editor_intro: Alarm templates by technology. Duplicate a built-in preset to customize, or import/export between projects.
monitor_preset_editor_duplicate_exists: A preset with that identifier already exists.
monitor_preset_matrix_hint: The project uses one alarm threshold preset (Normal, Strict, or custom). Click «Configure thresholds…» to view and edit the alarm matrix.
monitor_policy_editor_intro: Each row defines which condition triggers which severity. Columns: Comment (informational), Minor, Warning and Critical.
monitor_preset_matrix_scroll_tip: Go to preset/threshold matrix (F5 advanced dialog)
monitor_freq_manager_ref_need_play: Start capture (Play) and wait for stable measurement before fixing reference.
monitor_alarmas_intro:
INVENTORY SUPERVISION

Tree showing each channel status. Click → locate on spectrum. Right-click → context menu (supervise, acknowledge, thresholds, etc.).

ENABLE / DISABLE SUPERVISION

• Frequency manager: Sup column.
• Tree: right-click on channel or branch.
Both stay in sync. Unsupervised channels appear dimmed and do not raise alarms.

SEVERITIES (threshold presets)

• Critical — serious incident (very low SNR/MER, digital sync lost, etc.).
• Minor — intermediate degradation.
• Warning — early notice.
• Comment — informational only; no operator action required.

ICONS AND COLORS

• Green — OK, no issues.
• Blinking red — active critical alarm, pending attention.
• Blinking yellow — active warning or minor, pending attention.
• Solid yellow — active alarm already acknowledged (condition may persist).
• Brown — informational comment.
• Latched — signal recovered; incident stays open until acknowledged.

ACKNOWLEDGING ALARMS

• Right-click channel → Acknowledge.
• F4 — acknowledge all pending.
• F9 — acknowledge selected channel.

Acknowledging confirms the operator has seen the incident: blinking stops if the alarm is still active, or the incident closes if it was latched. Comments do not require acknowledgement.

LOGGING (REC)

• REC button (red circle): start or stop a log session in its own folder (alarms.csv, session.json, report.txt).
• Can be enabled without Play; the session stays empty until capture is running.
• Stopping REC automatically writes CSV and TXT in the session folder.
• Clock: elapsed time while recording; after stop, start/end/duration of the last session.
• Settings icon: log folder, export folder, and trigger mode (manual / Play / automatic).

STATUS BAR

Bottom bar: project path | green/orange/red (n) indicators — click opens alarms | REC · clock · last log · settings | workspace.

TOOLBAR

Floating window · Thresholds · Group · Locate · REC · clock · settings · last log · (i) this help.

SHORTCUTS: F3 = Alarms · F4 = ack all · F5 = thresholds · F8 = locate · F9 = ack selection.
monitor_alarmas_rules_hint: SNR-over-noise thresholds. Scopes: project, zone, device type, manufacturer, model, or channel. F1 = help · F5 = edit thresholds.
monitor_alarmas_engine_pending: Press Play to measure live alarms and log events.
monitor_supervision_events_intro: Project alarm and acknowledgement history. Stored in SQLite and, when active, in the session CSV.
monitor_alarm_cause_mer_warning: {label} ({freq} MHz): MER {snr} dB below warning ({threshold} dB)
monitor_alarm_cause_mer_critical: {label} ({freq} MHz): MER {snr} dB below critical ({threshold} dB)
monitor_alarm_cause_snr_warning: Channel {label} ({freq} MHz): SNR {snr} dB below warning threshold ({threshold} dB)
monitor_alarm_cause_snr_critical: Channel {label} ({freq} MHz): SNR {snr} dB below critical threshold ({threshold} dB)
monitor_alarm_cause_snr_recovered: Channel {label} ({freq} MHz): SNR recovered above threshold
monitor_alarm_cause_ack_manual: Channel {label} ({freq} MHz): manually acknowledged by operator
monitor_alarm_cause_ack_manual_active: Channel {label} ({freq} MHz): active alarm manually acknowledged
monitor_alarm_cause_ack_auto: Channel {label} ({freq} MHz): reset by automatic timer
monitor_supervision_tree_intro: Compact view of all inventory channels. Click to locate on spectrum; right-click to enable/disable supervision, acknowledge alarms, and more.
monitor_supervision_log_view_intro: Events recorded in the current session since the CSV log was started.
monitor_supervision_log_view_intro_scope: Events for “{scope}” in the current session since the CSV log was started.
monitor_supervision_log_view_empty: No log entries for this selection.
monitor_supervision_log_view_no_session: No active log. Start capture with CSV logging (Play or automatic session).
monitor_supervision_scope_freq_intro: Set the threshold preset for each frequency. Changes sync with the frequency manager.
monitor_thresholds_preset_intro: Select a preset and configure which condition triggers each severity (Comment, Minor, Warning, Critical). Normal and Strict are base templates: use «Save as…» to customize.
monitor_thresholds_delete_active: Cannot delete the project's active preset. Activate another preset first.
monitor_thresholds_fundamental_hint: The four base presets (Normal/Strict FM and DIG) cannot be deleted or overwritten. Edit the matrix and use «Save as…» to create a custom preset.
monitor_thresholds_dialog_intro: Unified threshold preset: each row defines an RF condition and the severity it triggers (Comment, Minor, Warning, Critical). FM ignores MER/sync on analog gear; DIG evaluates them. The four base presets are read-only — save as a copy to customize.
monitor_thresholds_fundamental_save_bloc: Normal and Strict cannot be overwritten. Use «Save as…» to save changes under a new name.
monitor_thresholds_fundamental_ok_prompt: You changed a base template (Normal or Strict). Use «Save as…» to keep your edits, or discard to apply only the active preset selection.
monitor_thresholds_intro: Set when warnings and critical alarms trigger based on SNR above local noise. Precedence: channel → model → manufacturer → device type → zone → project.
monitor_thresholds_global_hint: These thresholds apply to all channels without more specific rules.
monitor_thresholds_inherited_hint: Showing inherited values. OK saves a custom rule for this scope.
monitor_thresholds_effective: SNR warning {warning} dB · critical {critical} dB · debounce {debounce} ms · DIG {digital_on} · MER {mer_warning}/{mer_critical} dB · DIG debounce {digital_debounce} ms
monitor_thresholds_invalid_order: Critical threshold must be less than or equal to warning (stricter SNR).
monitor_thresholds_invalid_mer_order: Critical MER threshold must be less than or equal to warning.
monitor_thresholds_no_items: No inventory items for this scope.
monitor_supervision_help_intro: Supervision, alarms, REC, status bar, thresholds, and reports — operator guide.
monitor_shortcut_locate_none: Select a channel in the supervision tree to locate (F8).
monitor_shortcut_ack_none: Select a channel with a pending alarm to acknowledge (F9).
monitor_rf_gain_high_sum: High IF+BB gain ({sum_db} dB) — LNA {lna} · VGA {vga}. Reduce if you see distortion.
monitor_rf_gain_clamped: RF gain adjusted — LNA {lna} dB · VGA {vga} dB · P {preamp}
monitor_export_no_trace: No trace to export. Press Start and wait for data.
monitor_export_csv_workbench_tip: MHz,dBm CSV with no header — Shure Wireless Workbench scan import
monitor_export_csv_soundbase_tip: CSV with Frequency_MHz,Level_dBm header — Shure SoundBase scan upload
monitor_sdr_span_clamped: Analyzer SPAN saved · SDR limited to {span} MHz (instant BW)
monitor_alert_manual_trigger: Manual trigger — press Trigger to refresh trace
monitor_alert_no_data: No data from device — check USB or press Start
monitor_tip_status_edit_rbw: RBW — click for menu (auto / manual / presets)
monitor_tip_status_edit_vbw: VBW — click for menu (auto / manual / presets)
monitor_full_span_sweep_hint: Full span — panoramic sweep (reduce SPAN for detail)
monitor_radio_hint_analyzer: Analyzer mode: carrier supervision built in (markers M2). No audio.
monitor_radio_receive_tip: Demodulation mode: WFM (broadcast FM), NFM (land mobile/mics), AM, DSB, or DIG (constellation). SDR mode with Play only.
monitor_radio_bandwidth_tip: Channel filter in the demod chain (±BW/2 around the VFO), not the spectrum SPAN/BW. WFM: 150–250 kHz (broadcast channel). NFM: 6–25 kHz. Fixed steps per mode; you can also type a value.
monitor_radio_snap_interval_tip: VFO tuning grid: arrow keys and spectrum clicks snap to multiples of this step (e.g. 100 kHz for broadcast FM). You can type any frequency manually; Snap applies when using arrows or clicking the plot.
monitor_radio_deemphasis_tip: FM de-emphasis filter after the discriminator. European WFM: 50 µs. North America: 75 µs. NFM: usually none.
monitor_radio_noise_blanker_tip: Noise blanker on the FM discriminator output (SDR++ IF noise reduction). 0 = off.
monitor_radio_wfm_stereo_tip: Decode 19 kHz pilot and 38 kHz (L−R) subcarrier for broadcast WFM stereo audio.
monitor_radio_wfm_rds_tip: Extract PI and PS name from the 57 kHz RDS subcarrier. May take a few seconds to sync.
monitor_radio_wfm_lowpass_tip: WFM audio low-pass filter (~15 kHz). Keep enabled for clean broadcast FM.
monitor_radio_iq_correction_tip: Basic IQ correction (DC + I/Q balance) before demodulation.
monitor_radio_iq_invert_tip: Invert I/Q (complex conjugate). Use if demodulation sounds inverted.
monitor_radio_bias_tee_tip: HackRF Bias-T (antenna power). Leave OFF unless using a coax-fed active preamp.
monitor_radio_squelch_tip: Squelch threshold in dBFS (demodulated signal level). Red line shows live level. Lower the threshold to open on weak signals; raise it to cut noise.
monitor_radio_info_body:
SDR mode: demodulation and audio for the tuned channel.

Tuning FC vs F (frequency bar):
• FC — demodulates at screen center (blue FC line). Dragging pans the spectrum; audio follows the center.
• F — demodulates at the active marker (M1…M10). Drag the marker or use VFO; the green band shows demod bandwidth.

Only one controls audio: FC uses center; F uses the active marker. Do not mix both as references.

AUTO: optimizes IQ span, LNA/VGA/P gains and WFM profile for best quality.
FM Broad: FM broadcast preset (2 MHz, WFM 200 kHz, stereo/RDS).
Squelch: mutes audio below the dBFS threshold.
monitor_radio_vfo_tip: Tuned frequency for demodulation (Hz). Must fall within the visible IQ span. Center on a station with F on the spectrum or enter the frequency here. Arrow keys use the Snap step.
monitor_radio_squelch_rf_tip: Squelch threshold in dBm (RF level at the VFO). The slider red line shows the live measured signal.
monitor_radio_show_demod_bw_tip: Shades ±BW/2 centered on demod F/VFO in the spectrum (fill only, no edge lines).
monitor_radio_hint_spectrum: Pure analyzer mode: spectrum and waterfall without audio. Best for measurement and overview.
monitor_radio_hint_sdr: SDR mode: demodulation and audio to diagnose a single channel (analog FM mics).
monitor_radio_hint_dig: DIG mode: constellation and EVM/MER. DAB+ auto-detected in Band III (174–240 MHz). Use AUTO for RF gains.
monitor_radio_digital_active: Digital analysis active — see constellation below.
monitor_digital_select_dig: Select DIG in Receive to analyze digital signals.
monitor_radio_hint_supervision: Show supervision mode: inventory markers and alarms (M2). Audio disabled.
monitor_radio_audio_pending: Audio at {freq} — output not implemented yet.
monitor_demod_waiting: Waiting for IQ — press Play in SDR mode.
monitor_fm_broadcast_tip: SDR++ WFM params at current tune (does not change MHz): 2 MHz span, LNA 40, VGA 18, preamp ON, IF NR, BW 200 kHz, snap 100 kHz, 50 µs de-emphasis, low-pass ON.
monitor_cfg_debug_locked: DEBUG section is protected. Click the lock and enter the administrator password to view setup and diagnostics.
monitor_auto_tune_tip: Optimizes IQ span, LNA/VGA/P gains and WFM profile (snap, de-emphasis, IF NR, stereo/RDS) for best quality on the current station.
monitor_auto_tune_not_sdr: Available only in SDR mode with capture running.
monitor_mode_warn_span_analyzer: In SDR mode the maximum span is {max_mhz:.0f} MHz. Switch to Analyzer for wider sweeps.
monitor_mode_warn_full_span_analyzer: Full device span requires Analyzer mode (SDR is limited to {max_mhz:.0f} MHz).
monitor_mode_warn_demod_sdr_only: Demodulation and audio are only available in SDR mode. Press SDR on the toolbar.
monitor_mode_warn_title: Action not available in this mode
monitor_auto_tune_no_frame: Wait for a spectrum trace before using AUTO.
monitor_auto_tune_no_signal: No measurable signal at the VFO — check frequency and antenna.
monitor_auto_tune_disabled: Demod AUTO is temporarily disabled (under review).
monitor_digital_analysis_hint: DAB+: sync OFDM, ensemble y EVM. Audio vía welle.io (welle-cli).
monitor_dab_welle_hint: Para audio DAB+ instale welle.io (welle-cli) con SoapySDR+HackRF. Detenga ControladoRF antes de decodificar.
monitor_dab_welle_btn_launch_tip: Stops ControladoRF and launches welle-cli with HackRF (web UI on localhost).
monitor_dab_welle_confirm_body: welle-cli needs exclusive access to the HackRF. Stop ControladoRF capture and open welle-cli?
monitor_digital_idle: Enable digital analysis with IQ capture.
monitor_radio_supervision_pending: Inventory markers and thresholds — coming soon (M2).
monitor_rf_quality_hint: Metrics at marker F/FC frequency. Valid for FM and digital wireless (RF layer).
monitor_rf_quality_hint_wfm: FM channel RF metrics (power, OBW, SNR, ACP). No digital modulation or symbol rate — use RDS for station metadata.
monitor_rf_sdr_only: RF quality available in SDR mode only.
monitor_radio_spectrum_mode_banner: Not available in spectrum mode. Switch to SDR mode to demodulate audio and use these controls.
monitor_rf_supervision_off: RF metrics disabled in supervision mode.
monitor_source_status_idle: Stopped. Select source and start capture.
monitor_setup_intro: SDR setup assistant (Windows / macOS / Linux). SDR++-compatible sources: HackRF, Airspy, Airspy HF+.
monitor_setup_ready_yes: Yes — you can start capture (or use Simulation)
monitor_setup_no_steps_needed: No pending steps for this device.
monitor_setup_wizard_body: CONTROLADORF detects hardware over USB and checks drivers, CLI tools and backends. HackRF One is the project default device.
monitor_setup_wizard_hint: After installing, click Check again in the SOURCE panel. Use Simulation meanwhile.
monitor_setup_hackrf_win_driver_detail: Download HackRF tools or PothosSDR and install libhackrf USB utilities.
monitor_setup_hackrf_win_path_detail: Add the bin folder (hackrf_info.exe) to PATH or set HACKRF_LIB_DIR.
monitor_setup_hackrf_python_detail: pip install python_hackrf — requires libhackrf installed first.
monitor_setup_hackrf_mac_brew_detail: brew install hackrf — includes hackrf_info and libhackrf.
monitor_setup_hackrf_linux_pkg_detail: Install hackrf and libhackrf from your distribution.
monitor_setup_hackrf_linux_udev_detail: Copy 53-hackrf.rules and reload udev for non-root access.
monitor_setup_airspy_win_driver_detail: Install the official Airspy/Zadig package if Windows does not recognize the device.
monitor_setup_airspy_tools_detail: Install airspy_info and libairspy; add bin to PATH.
monitor_setup_airspy_linux_udev_detail: Add udev rules for Airspy VID/PID (1d50:60a1).
monitor_setup_airspy_linux_udev_cmd: sudo sh -c 'echo SUBSYSTEM==\"usb\", ATTR{idVendor}==\"1d50\", ATTR{idProduct}==\"60a1\", MODE=\"0666\"' > /etc/udev/rules.d/52-airspy.rules
monitor_setup_airspyhf_win_driver_detail: Install the HF+ package from airspy.com.
monitor_setup_airspyhf_mac_brew_detail: brew install airspy — recent builds include HF+ support.
monitor_tip_fc: FC fixed at center — horizontal drag pans the spectrum (hand cursor)
monitor_tip_fc_f: Marker F: horizontal drag moves the selected frequency
monitor_tip_fc_menu: Frequency options: FC/F, step, pan mode
monitor_tip_rbw: HackRF sweep RBW — frequency step per sweep (≥ 100 kHz). Manual: Hz presets.
monitor_tip_rbw_iq: Resolution bandwidth (sample rate ÷ FFT). Linked to the FFT control in IQ mode.
monitor_tip_rbw_menu: Auto or manual RBW — flat preset list with checkmark
monitor_tip_fft: FFT points (SDR++/HackRF IQ) — display resolution = SR ÷ FFT. More points = finer trace.
monitor_tip_smooth: Trace smoothing — merges adjacent bins (×N). OFF = raw trace. Does not change RF capture.
monitor_tip_smooth_menu: OFF or manual ×3 … ×51 presets (check on active)
monitor_tip_sweep_iq: Not applicable in IQ mode (continuous FFT). SWT is used in wide-span sweep only.
monitor_tip_sweep_menu: Manual/auto sweep, single shot and trigger
monitor_tip_span_iq_sr: HackRF sample rate (Msps). Auto IF filter ~75% of SR (e.g. 1.75 MHz at 2 Msps)
monitor_tip_span_menu: Span mode: manual, full, zero, last
monitor_tip_ampt_menu: AMPT scale: AUTO, units, offset, range
monitor_tip_lna: HackRF IF gain (-l): 0–40 dB, 8 dB steps (MAX2837)
monitor_tip_preamp: HackRF RF amp (-a): off or ~11 dB at antenna
monitor_tip_vga: HackRF BB gain (-g): 0–62 dB, 2 dB steps (MAX2837)
monitor_tip_overlay_span: Adjust SPAN (log scale). Blue shadow shows the visible window; wheel: discrete steps.
monitor_tip_overlay_span_sdr: Adjust BW (2–20 MHz, log scale). Shadow = tuned window; wheel: discrete steps.
monitor_tip_span_viewport: Device tunable range; the blue rectangle is the current SPAN window (read-only).
monitor_dock_pin_pinned: Pinned — panel always visible (click for auto-collapse)
monitor_tip_status_edit_start: Click to edit F start (left edge of axis)
monitor_tip_status_edit_center: Click to edit F center (FC) — keeps SPAN
monitor_tip_status_edit_stop: Click to edit F stop (right edge of axis)
monitor_tip_status_readout: FC/F mode — click for frequency menu
monitor_tip_status_edit_ref: Reference — click to edit or enable Auto
monitor_tip_status_edit_res: Vertical resolution — click to edit or enable Auto
monitor_tip_status_edit_preamp: +14 dB preamp — click to toggle ON/OFF
monitor_tip_status_edit_vga: VGA / IF gain (0–62 dB, 2 dB steps)
monitor_status_dialog_rf_hint: LNA: first RF amp. VGA: IF gain. P: ~14 dB preamp.
monitor_status_dialog_start_hint: Sets the left edge. F stop is kept; SPAN and FC update.
monitor_status_dialog_stop_hint: Sets the right edge. F start is kept; SPAN and FC update.
monitor_status_dialog_span_hint_iq: In IQ mode minimum span is 2 MHz (HackRF limit). Narrower spans use sweep mode automatically.
monitor_status_dialog_span_hint_sweep: Sweep mode: fine resolution, spans from 100 kHz.
monitor_status_dialog_ref_hint: Turns off vertical AUTO scale when confirmed.
monitor_tip_trigger: Manual trigger: freezes trace until you press (Manual mode in SWT menu)
monitor_tip_capture_trace: Freeze trace as max-hold (peak per bin)
monitor_tip_status_edit_bandwidth: HackRF sample rate (IQ bandwidth, SDR++ equivalent)
monitor_tip_status_edit_fi_filter: HackRF IF bandpass filter (auto or manual: 1.75 / 2.5 / … MHz)
monitor_status_dialog_bandwidth_hint: HackRF IQ sample rate in MHz (e.g. 2 MHz). Same as Bandwidth in SDR++.
monitor_tip_bw_sdr: HackRF sample rate (Msps). Auto IF filter ≈75% (e.g. 1.75 MHz at 2 Msps, like SDR++)
view_reset_panels_done: Panels restored to the default layout.
items_panel_hint: Sample item management. Data is stored in the SQLite database.
items_msg_no_services: Application services unavailable. Restart the application.
items_msg_select_row: Select a row in the table.
about_tagline: RF inventory and live-show spectrum monitoring
about_features_html:
• J. Alberto Velasco
  • Global and per-workspace configuration
  • Automatic state synchronization
  • Help menu and About dialog
  • Custom application icon
ws_error_create: Could not create workspace. Invalid or duplicate name.
ws_error_no_active_duplicate: There is no active workspace to duplicate.
ws_error_duplicate: Could not duplicate workspace. Invalid or duplicate name.
ws_error_duplicate_access: Could not access the new workspace after duplication.
ws_error_delete_default: The default workspace cannot be deleted.
ws_error_import: Could not import workspace. Invalid file or duplicate name.
workbench_import_replace_requires_projec: Open or create a project before replacing inventory.
structure_dialog_hint: View of the .crf document: metadata, modules, imported inventory, and panel layout.
inventory_properties_empty: Select a channel in the list to view its properties.
inventory_actions_hint: Actions on the open project's RF inventory.
inventory_actions_empty: Actions for the selected channel will be defined here.
inventory_locked_message: This item is locked or inherits lock from list/group. Unlock it to edit or delete.
inventory_locked_edit_hint: This item is locked. Uncheck Lock and click Apply to edit.
inventory_locked_inherited_hint: Lock inherited from list or group. Unlock the source to edit.
inventory_locked_edit_blocked: Item is locked. Unlock it in Properties before editing.
inventory_properties_list_subtitle: Metadata for the entire RF list
inventory_actions_future_hint: Coordination and monitoring actions linked to other app modules will appear here.
inventory_export_summary: Project «{project}» · {count} channels. Only the RF list will be exported with each channel's properties and list metadata.
inventory_export_hint: CSV and JSON include all fields. PDF adds a detailed sheet per channel at the end (RF, device, coordination, metadata and IDs).
monitor_alarmas_rec_clock_tip: REC session duration or last session summary
monitor_status_supervision_ok: OK channels — click to open alarms
monitor_status_supervision_warning: Warnings / minor — click to open alarms
